ud2.5:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud2.5:1.3: And on that occasion a certain lay follower from Icchānaṅgalaka had arrived in Sāvatthī on some business affairs.
ud2.5:1.4:
Having settled his affairs in Sāvatthī, he went to the Blessed One and,
on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
ud2.5:1.5: As he was sitting there, the Blessed One said to him,
ud2.5:1.6: “At long last you have managed to come here.”
ud2.5:2.1:
“For a long time, lord, have I wanted to come see the Blessed One,
but being involved in one business affair after another, I have not been able to do so.”
ud2.5:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud2.5:4.1: How blissful it is, for one who has nothing,
ud2.5:4.2: who has mastered the Dhamma, is learned.
ud2.5:4.3: See him suffering, one who has something,
ud2.5:4.4: a person bound in body with people.
